payload.title: Operator-surface input needs capability and submit-strategy modeling
payload.summary: A live attempt to send next into a bound builder operator surface showed that identity-targeted focus and text delivery can work, but submit semantics are not portable. Enter, Ctrl+Enter, and Shift+Enter inserted newlines; Alt+Enter triggered Windows Terminal fullscreen/focus behavior. Narada operator-surface input machinery should model type and submit as separate capabilities rather than assuming generic keyboard chords.
evidence: Runtime binding for narada-andrey.Bob resolved to a single WindowsTerminal HWND and text next was delivered to the intended surface.
Enter, Ctrl+Enter, and Shift+Enter all produced additional newlines instead of submitting.
Alt+Enter changed Windows Terminal window state rather than submitting to the agent.
proposal: Introduce operator-surface input capabilities: focus, type_text, submit, clear_pending_input, and recover_surface_state.
Represent submit_strategy per surface with postures such as type_only, operator_confirmed_submit, and known_surface_submit.
Default automation should be type_only until a submit strategy has explicit surface-specific evidence and authority.
Add guardrails that prevent repeated blind submit-chord probing against live agent surfaces.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as a Narada proper product task or doctrine addition for operator-surface action posture.
